prompt:
Our cannery floor-report tool won't build since someone reorganized the source tree into packages last week (labels got their own package, some files got moved around, apparently in a hurry before vacation). CI just runs `bash run_verify.sh`, which compiles everything and runs the checks. Full compiler output:

  ./cannery/batch/SizeGrader.java:4: error: class Grader is public, should be declared in a file named Grader.java
  public class Grader {
         ^
  ./cannery/batch/CookLog.java:7: error: duplicate class: cannery.CookLog
  public class CookLog {
         ^
  ./TestMain.java:4: error: cannot access CookLog
  import cannery.batch.CookLog;
                      ^
    bad source file: ./cannery/batch/CookLog.java
      file does not contain class cannery.batch.CookLog
      Please remove or make sure it appears in the correct subdirectory of the sourcepath.
  ./cannery/report/YieldReport.java:9: error: cannot find symbol
  import cannery.batch.Labeler;
                      ^
    symbol:   class Labeler
    location: package cannery.batch
  ./cannery/report/YieldReport.java:14: error: cannot find symbol
      private final Labeler labeler = new Labeler();
                    ^
    symbol:   class Labeler
    location: class YieldReport
  ./TestMain.java:32: error: cannot find symbol
          CookLog log = new CookLog();
          ^
    symbol:   class CookLog
    location: class TestMain
  ./TestMain.java:32: error: cannot find symbol
          CookLog log = new CookLog();
                            ^
    symbol:   class CookLog
    location: class TestMain
  ./cannery/report/YieldReport.java:14: error: cannot find symbol
      private final Labeler labeler = new Labeler();
                                          ^
    symbol:   class Labeler
    location: class YieldReport
  8 errors

What throws me is that half of these point at files nobody touched — TestMain.java is complaining and that file is frozen. Also heads up that there seem to be two graders in the batch package now and I genuinely do not know which one is current; QA signed off on the June thresholds and the tests encode those, so trust the tests over anything else you find lying around.

Get `bash run_verify.sh` green. Sort out the tree properly — no hacks like squeezing everything into one package. TestMain.java, run_verify.sh, and .gitignore are off limits.
notes: 5C3 item 2: build-order/classpath-free triage; the javac error stream is the prompt (captured verbatim from javac 26.0.1 via run_verify.sh, which compiles the sorted find-listing of all .java files — explicit file list is what makes every planted defect gate). Planted: (a) cannery/batch/CookLog.java declares `package cannery;` (moved without editing the statement) — errors surface AT the import in frozen TestMain.java ('bad source file') plus a duplicate-class report, cause is elsewhere; (b) cannery/report/YieldReport.java imports cannery.batch.Labeler but Labeler moved to cannery.label — cannot-find-symbol pair; (c) stale cannery/batch/SizeGrader.java still declares `public class Grader` (pre-June thresholds 12.0/4.0) alongside the current Grader.java (10.0/5.0) — 'should be declared in a file named Grader.java' error; the fix is deleting (or de-publicizing+unusing) the stale copy, and tests pin weights 11.0->case and 4.5->sample which distinguish the two threshold sets, so resolving the collision by keeping the stale body fails behaviorally. No chain-reveals observed: all 8 errors report in round 1 and the build is green after the three fixes. run_verify.sh writes out/ which .gitignore excludes from patches.
